title: arenaria filicaulis subsp. graeca
intro: Arenaria filicaulis subsp. graeca, also known as Greek sandwort, is a plant species within the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae) native to Greece and other parts of Europe. It is traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to offer health benefits such as reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and providing relief from gastrointestinal problems. In herbal preparations, the plant can be made into a tea or tincture, often used to treat various ailments, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
